rulings.Case Title: BENNETT GOLDBERG ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Stephens Institute'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2017 line 2. Defendant Stephens Institute'S Demurrer To Complaint Continued to July 10, 2017 to be heard in department 302 at 9:30 a.m. CCP 170.6 challenge to the Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay accepted.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANSELMO SAUCEDO ET AL VS. BISMARCK NUNEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2017 line 9. DEFENDANTs BISMARCK NUNEZ and SANDY FLORE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden and establish that there are not triable issues of fact and that Defendant is entitled to summary adjudication as a matter of law. Defendant fails to cite any law for the proposition that "notice of violation" per SFRO has to be attached to the complaint or its method of service be alleged. Additionally, Defendant fails to provide any evidence that SFRO 37.9(a)(2)(D) notice of violation has not been served. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PATEL RAJESHKUMAR VS. JAYCE MARTIN EVERTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Set Vacate Default And Judgment; Proposed Response; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT JAYCE EVERTS's Motion To Set Vacate Default And Judgment GRANTED, subject to (1) Defendant posting $12,540 with the Court no later than June 20, 2017 p.m.; and (2)Defendant filing answer no later than 4:00 p.m. on June 20, 2017.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. DARREN BOLDEN ET AL

DEFENDANT DARREN BOLDEN's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT GRANTED, subject to (1) Defendant filing and providing a courtesy copy of executed proper Proof of Service for the Motion; opposition, (2) opposition; (3) Defendant posting $636.76 with the Court no later than June 20, 2017 p.m.; and (4) Defendant filing answer no later than 4:00 p.m. on June 20, 2017.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAMELA TIEN VS. CATHERINE PAUL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2017 line 7. DEFENDANT CATHERINE PAUL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain.= (501/REQ)
